definition: The 3m10y yield spread's recession-prediction reliability is degraded when term premium is rising due to fiscal supply. The recent un-inversion occurred ~75% from term premium expansion rather than changed growth expectations, unlike the environment in which the model was calibrated.
evidence: ~150bp of the 200bp move from -150bp to +50bp reflects term premium, not growth expectations
The model was calibrated in an era of low, stable term premium
Logically sound analysis of the mechanism
Misinterpretation risk is actively present among market participants
open_questions: Has not been formally tested against the current regime
Historical out-of-sample validation limited given unprecedented fiscal configuration
What alternative recession indicators remain reliable under fiscal supply pressure?
